title: NTFS block-size cap still overestimates size for sparse files
body: platform.rs caps allocated size using blksize-based capping (referenced against issue #295) to avoid NTFS over-reporting, but for genuinely sparse files (e.g. VM disk images with big holes) dust still reports a size much closer to the logical file size than the actual disk usage `du` reports.

title: --ignore-directory only matches literal paths, not globs
body: `dust -X node_modules` works for an exact literal component, but `dust -X '**/target'` doesn't do what you'd expect — cli.rs's ignore_directory option appears to be a plain string/path match rather than a glob, unlike --ignore_all_in_file which does support patterns from a file.

title: Bind-mounted duplicate filesystem not skipped by -x on some platforms
body: With `-x`/`--limit_filesystem` set, dir_walker.rs is supposed to stay on one filesystem, but with a bind mount of the same underlying device mounted at two paths, both paths still get walked and their sizes double-counted in the total, at least on our container hosts.

title: --ignore_all_in_file patterns behave differently on Windows due to path separators
body: A pattern file used with `-I patterns.txt` that works fine on Linux (matching `foo/bar`) doesn't match the equivalent path on Windows because filter.rs seems to compare against paths using backslashes without normalizing the separator first.

title: Precedence between -n/--number_of_lines and terminal-height auto-detection is unclear
body: Running dust in a script with output piped to a file (no real terminal), does -n's absence fall back to some fixed default line count, or does display.rs still try to detect a terminal height and produce something unexpected? The help text says default is 'terminal_height' but doesn't explain the non-terminal case.
